content:
First, note that for any positive integers \(a,b\),

\[
\mathrm{lcm}(a,b)\;=\;\frac{a\,b}{\gcd(a,b)}, 
\qquad
\mathrm{lcm}(a,391)\;=\;\frac{a\cdot 391}{\gcd(a,391)}.
\]

We want
\[
\mathrm{lcm}(a,b)\;>\;\mathrm{lcm}(a,391)
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\frac{a\,b}{\gcd(a,b)} \;>\;\frac{a\cdot 391}{\gcd(a,391)}
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\frac{b}{\gcd(a,b)}>\frac{391}{\gcd(a,391)}.
\]

Since \(391=17\cdot 23\), for \(a<391\) the only possible values of \(\gcd(a,391)\) are \(1,17,23\).  We examine each:

1. If \(\gcd(a,391)=1,\) then the right‐hand side is \(391\), forcing
   \(\;b/\gcd(a,b)>391\;\), hence \(b\ge392\).  

2. If \(\gcd(a,391)=17,\) the right‐hand side is \(391/17=23\), so we need
   \[
     \frac{b}{\gcd(a,b)}>23.
   \]
   The smallest \(b\) that can do this is \(b=24\) (with, say, \(a=17\) so
   \(\gcd(17,24)=1\)).

3. If \(\gcd(a,391)=23,\) the right‐hand side is \(391/23=17\), so we need
   \[
     \frac{b}{\gcd(a,b)}>17.
   \]
   The smallest solution is \(b=18\) (take \(a=23\), then \(\gcd(23,18)=1\),
   and
   \[
     \mathrm{lcm}(23,18)=23\cdot 18=414
     >
     \mathrm{lcm}(23,391)=391).
   \]

Checking all smaller \(b<18\) fails in each case, so the minimal \(b\) is

\[
\boxed{18}.
\]
